title: MechanismArchetype
description: Schema for Mechanism Ascriptions — the logical causal unit. Defines the owning Structure, function label, and the Starlark rule. DM auto-derives Effectors and Receptors from the rule's AST. DM input = DM output (1:1). Tenant-defined archetypes extending Mechanism MUST reference this schema via allOf.

structure.description: ID reference to the owning Structure.

function.description: Capability label describing what the Mechanism does (e.g., 'UserValidation', 'PaymentRouting', 'OrderTransformation'). MUST be unique within the owning Structure's scope — two Mechanisms in the same Structure cannot share the same function.

rule.description:
Starlark source code defining the Mechanism's behavioral logic. Every Mechanism carries a rule — the definition-manager auto-derives Effectors and Receptors from the rule's AST at authoring time.

## Sandboxed environment
The rule body executes in a sandboxed Starlark environment exposing exactly: the `sys` namespace, injected host functions (`on`, `now`, `uuid7`, `fullmatch`, `search`), and Starlark built-ins. No other globals are permitted. No `load()` statements allowed. Execution budget: configurable max steps (default 100k).

## sys namespace
- `sys.id` — read-only property: the Mechanism's `id`.
- `sys.effect(archetype, data)` — the single output primitive. Returns a fluent builder.

## Event trigger (Receptor derivation)
- `on("ArchetypeName")` — MUST be the first executable statement. Declares the triggering Archetype and returns its payload. Auto-derives a Receptor typed by the named Archetype.

## Effect chain (fluent API)
- `sys.effect("Arch", data)` — fire-and-forget. Auto-derives an Effector (base EffectorArchetype).
- `sys.effect("Arch", data).by("EffectorArch")` — typed effector. Auto-derives an Effector (port type EffectorArch).
- `sys.effect("Arch", data).receive("FeedbackArch")` — closed-loop with base ports. Auto-derives Effector + Receptor.
- `sys.effect("Arch", data).receive("FeedbackArch").on("ReceptorArch")` — closed-loop, typed receptor. Auto-derives Effector + Receptor (port type ReceptorArch).
- `sys.effect("Arch", data).by("E").receive("F").on("R")` — full typing. Auto-derives Effector (port type E) + Receptor (port type R).

## Closed-loop vs open-loop
- `.receive()` present → closed-loop: auto-derives a feedback Receptor. Chain returns feedback payload.
- `.receive()` absent → open-loop / fire-and-forget: no feedback Receptor derived. Chain returns None.

## Port archetype qualification
- `.by("ArchName")` qualifies the Effector port type (tenant archetype extending EffectorArchetype). Omitted = base EffectorArchetype.
- `.on("ArchName")` qualifies the Receptor port type (tenant archetype extending ReceptorArchetype). Omitted = base ReceptorArchetype. Must follow `.receive()`.

## Injected host functions
- `on(archetype)` — event trigger (see above).
- `now()` → ISO 8601 timestamp.
- `uuid7()` → UUIDv7 string (RFC 9562, time-sortable).
- `fullmatch(pattern, string)` → boolean (full regex match).
- `search(pattern, string)` → first regex match or None.

## Constraints
- MUST begin with exactly one `on("...")` call as first executable statement.
- All archetype name arguments in `sys.effect()` chains MUST be string literals (enables static analysis and auto-derivation).
- Chain order: `effect` → [`by`] → [`receive` → [`on`]]. Each method appears at most once.
- All archetype name literals MUST resolve to declared Archetypes in the governed scope.
